channel.title: David Mausolf
channel.description: Essays on work, AI, and who actually produces the outcomes.

item.title: The Two-Person Growth Org
item.description: Most growth organizations are staffed for a complexity that no longer exists. The future needs two kinds of leadership, not twenty roles. On the architecture of the compressed growth org.
item.dc:creator: David Mausolf

item.title: Governed Teams, Ungoverned Systems
item.description: Every team hits their number. The company still misses. The loss lives in a layer nobody built governance for. Inside Palantir's Forward Deployed Engineer model and why competitors can't follow.
item.dc:creator: David Mausolf

item.title: Who’s Actually Doing the Work?
item.description: Data-driven essay examining how organizations systematically overvalue visible work and undervalue the people who produce outcomes. Covers the introvert earnings gap, neurodivergent employment barriers, AI-driven compression of middle management, and the coming repricing of knowledge work.
item.dc:creator: David Mausolf
